probes.name: schedule_daily_recurring_job
probes.prompt: Hey, can you set up a recurring job that runs a daily research digest every morning at 8:00? Just the usual persona, twily_chat. I want it to collect any new stuff from my watched topics and send me a summary.
probes.criteria:
The cron_master agent's role is managing recurring and one-time scheduled jobs via schedule_manager.py. A good response (1) first obtains the current time, (2) schedules a recurring daily job at 08:00 using schedule_manager, (3) defaults the agent to persona/twily_chat, (4) confirms the schedule details back to the user including time, frequency, agent, and task description, and (5) does not fabricate execution results.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #7 (checking things since a date), #11 (collect conclusions), #20 (Add them also to calendar) — user habit of asking for scheduled/recurring information gathering.

probes.name: list_all_active_cron_jobs
probes.prompt: Can you show me all the cron jobs I currently have scheduled in the system? I want to see which ones are recurring, which are one-shot, and when they're set to fire next.
probes.criteria:
The cron_master agent's role is managing scheduled jobs. A good response (1) queries schedule_manager.py for all active jobs, (2) presents them in a clear list showing job name/type (recurring vs one-time), next fire time, and target agent, and (3) does not invent jobs that do not exist — if the list is empty, says so.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #13 (get me a list of tasks), #23 (Can you check the current tasks that are in the system?), #28 (What else is on my todo list?) — user frequently asks for system state overviews.

probes.name: view_recent_execution_history
probes.prompt: Oki, can you check what scheduled jobs actually ran in the last couple of days? I feel like something might have been skipped. Show me the execution history so I can see what fired and what didn't.
probes.criteria:
The cron_master agent's role includes viewing execution history. A good response (1) first gets the current time, (2) queries execution logs for recent runs (last ~48-72 hours), (3) clearly shows which jobs ran successfully, which failed, and which were skipped, with timestamps, and (4) if no history exists, states that honestly.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.
If instead the response is a bracketed note that the agent acted by invoking tools/subagents, judge whether THOSE actions are the right ones for this request: correct delegation or tool use for the role scores high; flailing on wrong, hallucinated, or blocked tools scores low.
TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #21 (I still see tasks that should have been marked as done), #26 (It seems it works quite nicely) — user monitors whether automated processes actually completed.

probes.name: polish_schedule_one_time_reminder
probes.prompt: Hej, możesz ustawić jednorazowego joba na jutro około 18:00 żeby mi przypomniał o wysłaniu tego maila z wnioskami? Tak z twily_chat persona. Będę wdzięczny.
probes.criteria:
The cron_master agent's role is managing scheduled jobs including one-time tasks. A good response (1) gets the current time to calculate the correct target, (2) creates a one-time job for tomorrow ~18:00, (3) uses persona/twily_chat as the default agent, (4) confirms the schedule details in Polish to match the user's language, and (5) does not claim to have already sent the reminder.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #11 (collect conclusions and write email), #2/3/4 (Polish/English mix, casual 'Oki'), #15 (Can you fetch me) — user mixes Polish casually and gives specific timed requests.

probes.name: cancel_existing_scheduled_job
probes.prompt: Actually, scrap that daily research digest — I don't need it running anymore. Can you cancel it? And while you're at it, is there anything else scheduled that might be stale or redundant?
probes.criteria:
The cron_master agent manages recurring and one-time scheduled jobs. A good response (1) queries current jobs to find and remove/cancel the specified daily research digest job, (2) also reviews remaining active jobs for any that appear stale or redundant, (3) reports what was cancelled and any recommendations about other jobs, and (4) confirms cleanly without hallucinating jobs.
Score 0.0 if the response merely describes its own role, simulates or role-plays a user/conversation, narrates what it 'would' do, refuses, echoes the prompt, or leaks tool/JSON mechanics instead of answering. Reward a concrete, correct, on-task result a user could use as-is.

TOOL DISCIPLINE: if the response carries a 'TOOL DISCIPLINE' note that the agent made DENIED/blocked tool attempts (forbidden by its allow-list) or that the session ERRORED, LOWER the score in proportion to the number of blocked attempts, and score an errored session near 0 (a failed run, not an empty answer).
probes.source_hint: Inspired by messages #34 (this task is already done), #38 (so this is new list), #21 (tasks that should have been marked as done) — user frequently cleans up stale items and asks system to verify state.
